source: Dialog text only from day-1, day-2, day-3, day-4 content modules listed in docs/conversation-word-posts.json
normalization: Case-insensitive; curly apostrophes normalized to straight apostrophes; punctuation excluded; accented words count as one word.
annotation: Default senses come from docs/word-sense-defaults.json. Context-sensitive words use post-level sense-overrides.json files keyed by message index and token index.
hierarchy: Per-post study files live in docs/conversation-word-data/<post-id>/conversation-word-counts.json and conversation-word-senses.json.

examples: Today I tried to go to Joaquina, but I got on the wrong bus and ended up in Campeche instead.
Wait, you took the wrong bus and still found a beach? That sounds like a very lucky mistake.
It was lucky after the panic passed. At first, I looked out the window and realized the signs did not match my map.
Today I tried to go to Joaquina, but I got on the wrong bus and ended up in Campeche instead.

I asked a woman near the door, 'Is this bus going to Joaquina?' She smiled and said, 'No, Campeche.'

I asked a woman near the door, 'Is this bus going to Joaquina?' She smiled and said, 'No, Campeche.'
That is a good travel rule. If the wrong bus takes you to the ocean, maybe accept the gift.

At least you understood the important word. Did you know where Campeche was?
